title: Inconsistent Error Handling in Ingestion Command
description: The ingestion command emits actionable messages on errors but does not stop processing other inputs, which may lead to incomplete or inconsistent states.

root_cause_potential: Error handling does not halt processing on critical failures
Incomplete rollback mechanisms for failed operations
Lack of transactional behavior in ingestion pipeline
technical_analysis.suggested_solution: Ensure that critical errors halt the process or provide a clear rollback mechanism

reproduction_steps: 1. Start ingestion process with multiple files
3. Observe if processing continues with other files
4. Check system state for consistency after partial failure
expected_behavior: Critical errors should halt ingestion process and provide rollback capability to maintain data consistency
actual_behavior: Ingestion continues processing other inputs even after critical errors, potentially leaving system in inconsistent state

immediate_fixes.action: Implement proper error classification (critical vs non-critical)
immediate_fixes.rationale: Distinguish between errors that should halt processing vs those that can be skipped

immediate_fixes.action: Add rollback mechanism for failed ingestion operations
immediate_fixes.rationale: Ensure system can recover to consistent state after failures

immediate_fixes.action: Implement transactional behavior for batch operations
immediate_fixes.rationale: Ensure all-or-nothing semantics for multi-file ingestion

impact_assessment.user_impact: Medium - Data inconsistency risk during batch operations
